text.title: PM Certification Exam Changes 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the pm-certification-changes-2026 methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Diff MUST cite the official 2026 ECO PDF as primary source; secondary sources only as confirmation.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Diff MUST list explicit added / removed / renamed / reweighted tasks per domain — not summary prose.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Domain weights MUST be cited exactly (People 33% / Process 41% / Business Environment 26%).
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: All consumers MUST be reminded the change is effective 2026-07-01; pre-date sittings unaffected.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Diff MUST include a 'what to do differently' paragraph for candidates currently studying.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip for non-PMP certifications or when authoritative diff already published.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
